Features: Small stork, known for gap in dull gray/yellow bill. Pinkish legs.
Similar to Painted, but lacks Painted's pink tertials and large black patches on wings.
Similar to Milky, but has large black wing patch, pink near tail (tertials).
Massive rounded wings. Pale morph: dark brown, white breast with dark streaks; juvenile much paler. Dark morph: dark all over with paler outer feathers in flight.
Enormous and distinct raptor. Strong V in flight. White body and forewings, dark flight feathers.
Mostly brown and white. Stark contrast between brown body & white belly/tail. Broad black bar at end of tail. Juvenile paler with more streaks and barring. Forages near water.
Bright blue throat, orange-brown crown and nape.
Unique black "earmuffs." Dark above, white breast, rusty belly. Social, sometimes seen in small flocks.
Dark above, white below. All white belly and plain back distinct amongst fantails in range.
Male black and white below, scarlet from crown to rump on top. Female plain brown, but distinct bright red rump.
Olive back, yellow belly, white tail edges in flight. Female similar to Brown-throated, which has thicker bill and lacks white patches.
Very similar to Lesser. Larger. Male has more extensive black mask, no yellow on forehead. Female has yellow (not green) throat.
Very similar to Greater. Male has smaller black mask, traces of yellow on forehead. No blue on wings.
Vivid blue highlights on wings (unlike other leafbirds). Male has yellow-tinged head; female green headed with blue smudge on throat.
